# PuDialog

## Intent

- dialog
- confirmation dialog
- short focused workflow
- blocking decision

## Prefer When

- Use for confirmation flows, destructive decisions, and short modal workflows that benefit from built-in title, description, icon, and actions structure.

## Avoid When

- Avoid for fully custom overlay shells; use PuModal when the consumer should own most of the interior structure.
- Avoid for secondary side-panel workflows; use PuDrawer.

## Import

```ts
import { PuDialog } from '@partner-up-dev/ui-web'
```

## Props

- `id`
- `open`
- `title`
- `description`
- `ariaLabel`
- `closeLabel`
- `cancelText`
- `confirmText`
- `maxWidth`
- `tone`
- `showClose`
- `showCancel`
- `showConfirm`
- `cancelDisabled`
- `confirmDisabled`
- `confirmLoading`
- `closeOnOverlay`
- `closeOnEscape`
- `lockScroll`

## Slots

- `default`
- `actions`
- `description`
- `footer`
- `header`
- `icon`
- `title`

## Events

- `close`
- `cancel`
- `confirm`

## Composition

- PuHeader
- PuButton
- PuForm
- PuInlineNotice

## Story Variants

Story: `src/stories/overlay/PuDialog.story.vue`

- Confirmation
- Form Workflow
- Custom Slots
- Open State

## Types

- `PuDialogCloseReason`
- `PuDialogTone`

## Caveats

- Uses required open plus close, cancel, and confirm events; do not assume v-model:open.
- close emits a reason of overlay, escape, close, or cancel.
- Provide a useful title, title slot, or aria label for dialog context.
- Use the actions slot when custom commands are needed while preserving footer spacing.
